matches.lb: Quoting Commentary: Kli Yakar on Exodus 13:17:6
matches.x: A second approach to resolve these verses, is that the people mentioned here refers to the lesser elements among Israel and the mixed multitude [erev rav], for they share the same designation. When it says And it came to pass, when Pharaoh had let the people go, it speaks of the mixed multitude, for since he did not prevent them from leaving, it is as if he sent them away. We find the language of…

Inasmuch as a proper observance of the Sabbath signals and symbolizes a worshipful attitude, it would seem to follow that during periods of waywardness the people of God would naturally cease to comply with this law. Thus Nehemiah's reform included a reinstitution of a strict Sabbath observance. "In those days saw I in Judah some treading wine presses on the sabbath, and bringing in sheaves, and…

Upon returning from the captivity in Babylon, Ezra, a priest (and evidently high priest), and Nehemiah, the Jewish governor, also appointed the priests and Levites to officiate in "wards": "Thus cleansed I them from all strangers, and appointed the wards of the priests and the Levites, every one in his business." (Nehemiah 13:30. See also 12:24.) (John A. Tvedtnes, The Church of the Old…
